title: The Spectacular
author: Fiona Davis
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Spectacular" book by Fiona Davis?
Options:
A. After your mother left, I struggled as a single father trying to make sure you were happy. I should not have prevented her from seeing you just because she went after her dreams. Our family seemed perfect before she went to Boston, so I felt hurt when she left. But I know that was not right. I hope you understand I was focused entirely on being a good dad and providing for you both. Please forgive me for the mistakes I made.
B. And it wouldnt have been easy to get another. The corporate world doesnt like whistleblowers. I had the two of you to support, and I wanted to make sure you were happy, especially after Lucille left us. Thats all I was thinking about, my two darling girls. Please believe me. I know the truth about Moms time in Boston. You told her she couldnt see us, threatened her with full custody. All because she wanted to follow her dream. I shouldnt have done that. But we had a perfect family, why did she have to abandon us? I tried my best, after she left.
C. I was the sole provider for our family after your mother pursued her aspirations in Boston. I recognize now that prohibiting her from visiting you two was unethical, though at the time I believed our family was ideal before she deserted us. I wanted to guarantee your happiness after she left, which was my only priority. I hope you can forgive the mistakes I made then. 
D. It was challenging to find work after calling out unethical practices. I relied on that job to support our family, especially after your mom moved to Boston for her career. I wrongfully kept you from her because I felt she had abandoned our perfect family. I know now that was incorrect. I'm sorry. My only concern was caring for you girls after she left.
Answer:

options.A: After your mother left, I struggled as a single father trying to make sure you were happy. I should not have prevented her from seeing you just because she went after her dreams. Our family seemed perfect before she went to Boston, so I felt hurt when she left. But I know that was not right. I hope you understand I was focused entirely on being a good dad and providing for you both. Please forgive me for the mistakes I made.
options.B: And it wouldnt have been easy to get another. The corporate world doesnt like whistleblowers. I had the two of you to support, and I wanted to make sure you were happy, especially after Lucille left us. Thats all I was thinking about, my two darling girls. Please believe me. I know the truth about Moms time in Boston. You told her she couldnt see us, threatened her with full custody. All because she wanted to follow her dream. I shouldnt have done that. But we had a perfect family, why did she have to abandon us? I tried my best, after she left.
options.C: I was the sole provider for our family after your mother pursued her aspirations in Boston. I recognize now that prohibiting her from visiting you two was unethical, though at the time I believed our family was ideal before she deserted us. I wanted to guarantee your happiness after she left, which was my only priority. I hope you can forgive the mistakes I made then.
options.D: It was challenging to find work after calling out unethical practices. I relied on that job to support our family, especially after your mom moved to Boston for her career. I wrongfully kept you from her because I felt she had abandoned our perfect family. I know now that was incorrect. I'm sorry. My only concern was caring for you girls after she left.
